result.stdout:
Description:
  Various tools and utilities that I've needed or found useful.

Usage:
  Unlimitedinf.Tom [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  chart-system-stats <data-dir>                                      Given an input of system stat file(s), generate png charts for them.
  convert                                                            Convert one thing to another.
  hash <crc32|md5|sha1|sha256|sha512> <paths>                        Hashes files based on the chosen hash algorithm. [algorithm: MD5, paths: /tmp/inspectra-help-unlimitedinf.tom-5.1.5-bdf3ce9ec8b0420bbd143a186d5c7ff8]
  hash-rename <crc32|md5|sha1|sha256|sha512> <paths>                 Hashes files based on the chosen hash algorithm, and then renames the files to their hash. [algorithm: MD5, paths: /tmp/inspectra-help-unlimitedinf.tom-5.1.5-bdf3ce9ec8b0420bbd143a186d5c7ff8]
  identicon <seed> <output>                                          Generates a deterministic geometric identicon image from a string seed.
  img-dimension <paths>                                              Uses image dimensions to filter low-quality images into a separate folder.
                                                                     Filtered images will be placed in a 'toss' directory in the directy of the item(s) to inspect.
                                                                     If width and height are requested, then megapixels is ignored. Otherwise each filter will apply individually. [default: /tmp/inspectra-help-unlimitedinf.tom-5.1.5-bdf3ce9ec8b0420bbd143a186d5c7ff8]
  blockhash <confidence> <paths>                                     Uses blockhash to compute the hamming distance between image files. [confidence: 25, paths: /tmp/inspectra-help-unlimitedinf.tom-5.1.5-bdf3ce9ec8b0420bbd143a186d5c7ff8]
  random <Bit|Byte|Crumb|EightBall|Guid|Int|Long|Name|Nibble|Short>  Get random data.
  wsc <endpoint>                                                     Establish a web socket client connection to another instance of 'tom wss' for p2p communication.
  wss                                                                Creates a web socket server to be used for p2p communication.

commands.payload:
Description:
  Various tools and utilities that I've needed or found useful.

Usage:
  Unlimitedinf.Tom [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  chart-system-stats <data-dir>                                      Given an input of system stat file(s), generate png charts for them.
  convert                                                            Convert one thing to another.
  hash <crc32|md5|sha1|sha256|sha512> <paths>                        Hashes files based on the chosen hash algorithm. [algorithm: MD5, paths: /tmp/inspectra-help-unlimitedinf.tom-5.1.5-bdf3ce9ec8b0420bbd143a186d5c7ff8]
  hash-rename <crc32|md5|sha1|sha256|sha512> <paths>                 Hashes files based on the chosen hash algorithm, and then renames the files to their hash. [algorithm: MD5, paths: /tmp/inspectra-help-unlimitedinf.tom-5.1.5-bdf3ce9ec8b0420bbd143a186d5c7ff8]
  identicon <seed> <output>                                          Generates a deterministic geometric identicon image from a string seed.
  img-dimension <paths>                                              Uses image dimensions to filter low-quality images into a separate folder.
                                                                     Filtered images will be placed in a 'toss' directory in the directy of the item(s) to inspect.
                                                                     If width and height are requested, then megapixels is ignored. Otherwise each filter will apply individually. [default: /tmp/inspectra-help-unlimitedinf.tom-5.1.5-bdf3ce9ec8b0420bbd143a186d5c7ff8]
  blockhash <confidence> <paths>                                     Uses blockhash to compute the hamming distance between image files. [confidence: 25, paths: /tmp/inspectra-help-unlimitedinf.tom-5.1.5-bdf3ce9ec8b0420bbd143a186d5c7ff8]
  random <Bit|Byte|Crumb|EightBall|Guid|Int|Long|Name|Nibble|Short>  Get random data.
  wsc <endpoint>                                                     Establish a web socket client connection to another instance of 'tom wss' for p2p communication.
  wss                                                                Creates a web socket server to be used for p2p communication.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Uses blockhash to compute the hamming distance between image files.

Usage:
  Unlimitedinf.Tom blockhash [<confidence> [<paths>...]] [options]

Arguments:
  <confidence>  The confidence interval for blockhash similarity computed by hamming distance. [default: 25]
  <paths>       The directory path(s) to hash. The default is Environment.CurrentDirectory. [default: /tmp/inspectra-help-unlimitedinf.tom-5.1.5-bdf3ce9ec8b0420bbd143a186d5c7ff8]

Options:
  -q, --quiet     Do not display any extra information beyond the file hashes.
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Given an input of system stat file(s), generate png charts for them.

Usage:
  Unlimitedinf.Tom chart-system-stats <data-dir> [options]

Arguments:
  <data-dir>  The directory containing one or many files of data to use for generating the charts.

Options:
  --script        Instead of generating charts, display the script that should be installed to generate the data files.
  --merge         Instead of generating charts, merge any single line files into a single file.
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

  --merge         Instead of generating charts, merge any single line files into a single file.
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Convert one thing to another.

Usage:
  Unlimitedinf.Tom convert [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  base <source> <base>  Convert from base10 to baseX.

  Convert one thing to another.

Usage:
  Unlimitedinf.Tom convert [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  base <source> <base>  Convert from base10 to baseX.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Convert from base10 to baseX.

Usage:
  Unlimitedinf.Tom convert base <source> <base> [options]

Arguments:
  <source>  The source value.
  <base>    BaseX, where X is [2,36].

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Hashes files based on the chosen hash algorithm.

Usage:
  Unlimitedinf.Tom hash [<algorithm> [<paths>...]] [options]

Arguments:
  <crc32|md5|sha1|sha256|sha512>  The type of hash algorithm to use. [default: MD5]
  <paths>                         The directory path(s) to hash. The default is Environment.CurrentDirectory. [default: /tmp/inspectra-help-unlimitedinf.tom-5.1.5-bdf3ce9ec8b0420bbd143a186d5c7ff8]

Options:
  -q, --quiet     Do not display any extra information beyond the file hashes.
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Generates a deterministic geometric identicon image from a string seed.

Usage:
  Unlimitedinf.Tom identicon <seed> <output> [options]

Arguments:
  <seed>    The input string (e.g. an email address) to generate the identicon from.
  <output>  The output file path. Extension determines format: .svg or .png.

Options:
  -s, --size <size>      Output image size in pixels (square). [default: 64]
  -b, --blocks <blocks>  Number of cells along each side of the grid. [default: 4]
  -g, --grayscale        Use only the red channel value for all three channels.
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Generates a deterministic geometric identicon image from a string seed.

Usage:
  Unlimitedinf.Tom identicon <seed> <output> [options]

Arguments:
  <seed>    The input string (e.g. an email address) to generate the identicon from.
  <output>  The output file path. Extension determines format: .svg or .png.

Options:
  -s, --size <size>      Output image size in pixels (square). [default: 64]
  -b, --blocks <blocks>  Number of cells along each side of the grid. [default: 4]
  -g, --grayscale        Use only the red channel value for all three channels.
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Uses image dimensions to filter low-quality images into a separate folder.
  Filtered images will be placed in a 'toss' directory in the directy of the item(s) to inspect.
  If width and height are requested, then megapixels is ignored. Otherwise each filter will apply individually.

Usage:
  Unlimitedinf.Tom img-dimension [<paths>...] [options]

Arguments:
  <paths>  The directory path(s) to hash. The default is Environment.CurrentDirectory. [default: /tmp/inspectra-help-unlimitedinf.tom-5.1.5-bdf3ce9ec8b0420bbd143a186d5c7ff8]

Options:
  -w, --width <width>            The width of the image. [default: 0]
  -h, --height <height>          The height of the image. [default: 0]
  -m, --megapixels <megapixels>  The area of the image. Default of 0.5mp is equivalent to an approx 700x700 square image. [default: 0.5]
  -q, --quiet                    Do not display any extra information beyond the file hashes.
  -?, -h, --help                 Show help and usage information

  -q, --quiet                    Do not display any extra information beyond the file hashes.
  -?, -h, --help                 Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Get random data.

Usage:
  Unlimitedinf.Tom random <type> [options]

Arguments:
  <Bit|Byte|Crumb|EightBall|Guid|Int|Long|Name|Nibble|Short>  The type of random to get. Pick from the supported values.

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Establish a web socket client connection to another instance of 'tom wss' for p2p communication.

Usage:
  Unlimitedinf.Tom wsc <endpoint> [options]

Arguments:
  <endpoint>  The hostname and port to establish the websocket connection to. E.g wss://localhost:45678

Options:
  --working-dir <working-dir>                    The working directory to be used for serving files. Only files that are a children of this directory can be copied. [default: /tmp/inspectra-help-unlimitedinf.tom-5.1.5-bdf3ce9ec8b0420bbd143a186d5c7ff8]
  --ssl-cert-subject <ssl-cert-subject>          Used in scenarios where the HTTPS certificate is a valid and trusted SSL certificate but the remote name used to reach the destination won't match the presented certificate.
  --ssl-cert-thumbprint <ssl-cert-thumbprint>    Used in scenarios where the HTTPS certificate is a self-signed certificate and the only validation possible is by thumbprint.
  --max-session-duration <max-session-duration>  After this amount of time, the session will be aborted with the server. [default: 3.00:00:00]
  -?, -h, --help                                 Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Establish a web socket client connection to another instance of 'tom wss' for p2p communication.

Usage:
  Unlimitedinf.Tom wsc <endpoint> [options]

Arguments:
  <endpoint>  The hostname and port to establish the websocket connection to. E.g wss://localhost:45678

Options:
  --working-dir <working-dir>                    The working directory to be used for serving files. Only files that are a children of this directory can be copied. [default: /tmp/inspectra-help-unlimitedinf.tom-5.1.5-bdf3ce9ec8b0420bbd143a186d5c7ff8]
  --ssl-cert-subject <ssl-cert-subject>          Used in scenarios where the HTTPS certificate is a valid and trusted SSL certificate but the remote name used to reach the destination won't match the presented certificate.
  --ssl-cert-thumbprint <ssl-cert-thumbprint>    Used in scenarios where the HTTPS certificate is a self-signed certificate and the only validation possible is by thumbprint.
  --max-session-duration <max-session-duration>  After this amount of time, the session will be aborted with the server. [default: 3.00:00:00]
  -?, -h, --help                                 Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Creates a web socket server to be used for p2p communication.

Usage:
  Unlimitedinf.Tom wss [options]

Options:
  --working-dir <working-dir>              The working directory to be used for serving files. Only files that are a children of this directory can be copied. [default: /tmp/inspectra-help-unlimitedinf.tom-5.1.5-bdf3ce9ec8b0420bbd143a186d5c7ff8]
  --mbps <mbps>                            Rate-limit the sending and receiving of files to this megabits per second. [default: 0]
  --host <host>                            The hostname to run on. By default accepts any hostname. [default: +]
  -p, --port <port>                        The port to listen on for connections. [default: 54400]
  --https-pfx-path <https-pfx-path>        If provided, will use this certificate for HTTPS instead of the built-in ASP.NET Core certificate.
  --https-crtpem-path <https-crtpem-path>  If provided, will use this certificate for HTTPS instead of the built-in ASP.NET Core certificate. Needs to be paired with --https-keypem-path to work.
  --https-keypem-path <https-keypem-path>  If provided, will use this certificate for HTTPS instead of the built-in ASP.NET Core certificate. Needs to be paired with --https-crtpem-path to work.
  --https-generate-cert                    If provided, will generate an https certificate with a nonsense subject name to be used for manual validation.
  -?, -h, --help                           Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Creates a web socket server to be used for p2p communication.

Usage:
  Unlimitedinf.Tom wss [options]

Options:
  --working-dir <working-dir>              The working directory to be used for serving files. Only files that are a children of this directory can be copied. [default: /tmp/inspectra-help-unlimitedinf.tom-5.1.5-bdf3ce9ec8b0420bbd143a186d5c7ff8]
  --mbps <mbps>                            Rate-limit the sending and receiving of files to this megabits per second. [default: 0]
  --host <host>                            The hostname to run on. By default accepts any hostname. [default: +]
  -p, --port <port>                        The port to listen on for connections. [default: 54400]
  --https-pfx-path <https-pfx-path>        If provided, will use this certificate for HTTPS instead of the built-in ASP.NET Core certificate.
  --https-crtpem-path <https-crtpem-path>  If provided, will use this certificate for HTTPS instead of the built-in ASP.NET Core certificate. Needs to be paired with --https-keypem-path to work.
  --https-keypem-path <https-keypem-path>  If provided, will use this certificate for HTTPS instead of the built-in ASP.NET Core certificate. Needs to be paired with --https-crtpem-path to work.
  --https-generate-cert                    If provided, will generate an https certificate with a nonsense subject name to be used for manual validation.
  -?, -h, --help                           Show help and usage information
